metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the plan whether the test must set `REBAR_ROOT` / `repo_root` to the tmp store AND verify that a store-writing mutation injected into `_apply_completion_floor_to_verdict` (e.g., one that resolves `repo_root` from the environment) would actually land in the tmp store and be caught — or explicitly acknowledge that the fingerprint check is forward-looking and passes trivially for the current pure implementation.
findings.finding: `_apply_completion_floor_to_verdict` takes no `repo_root` parameter and is purely in-memory (verified by reading the implementation). The plan's AC step 1 — 'assert NO store mutation escaped' by comparing tickets-branch HEAD sha before/after — will therefore pass VACUOUSLY: the store is structurally unreachable by the function under test, so before==after is guaranteed regardless of what the floor actually does. The plan does not acknowledge this, leaving AC step 3 ('the test fails if a signing/transition/reopen/event-append is introduced') potentially unfalsifiable unless the mutation check is designed to inject a side-effectful call that REACHES the store through some other path.
findings.location: Plan §What, steps 1/4 and AC line: 'Assert NO store mutation escaped: the AFTER store fingerprint is byte-identical to BEFORE'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the plan whether `_partial_epic`'s `monkeypatch`-based store-read mocks are retained alongside the real tmp store (sentinel-only pattern) or replaced with real ticket data written to the store — these produce fundamentally different test architectures.
findings.finding: The plan claims the new test uses `_partial_epic` ('the existing `_partial_epic` gold fixture') inside a real temporary rebar store, but `_partial_epic` is a `monkeypatch`-parameterized helper that replaces all store reads (`rebar._reads.list_tickets`, `rebar._reads.show_ticket`, `attest.delivered_now`) with lambdas. When monkeypatching bypasses the store, `rebar.init_repo` bootstraps a real store that is never queried — the store fingerprint before/after is trivially identical. The plan does not specify how the real store and the monkeypatched reads coexist or which is authoritative, leaving the test design ambiguous for implementors.
findings.location: Plan §What step 1: 'Create a real rebar store in a tmp dir (rebar.init_repo)… then run the floor over the existing _partial_epic gold fixture'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether AC step 2 should target `_maybe_apply_completion_floor` (which accepts `repo_root` and can be pointed at the real tmp store) rather than the pure `_apply_completion_floor_to_verdict`, so the real-store fingerprint guard is non-vacuous.
findings.finding: _apply_completion_floor_to_verdict (the function the plan names in AC step 2) is documented as 'Pure (no LLM)' and accepts no repo_root parameter — it has zero I/O pathways to the store. Creating a real rebar store via rebar.init_repo and diffing its HEAD sha / event-file inventory before/after calling this function will trivially always pass (the store is structurally unreachable from this call). The plan's claimed benefit — 'catches ANY signing / transition / reopen / event-append … regardless of how that call is imported' — requires the tested surface to accept repo_root; that is _maybe_apply_completion_floor, not _apply_completion_floor_to_verdict.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace step 2's call target with `plan_review._maybe_apply_completion_floor(ticket_id, verdict, ctx=..., cfg=..., runner=..., repo_root=tmp_repo_root)` (pointing repo_root at the tmp store and setting completion_floor_active=True in the cfg), which has the I/O pathway the mutation guard requires. Use monkeypatch only for the LLM runner call (_classify_completion) so the test stays unit-fast.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the plan whether the real-store mutation check tests `_maybe_apply_completion_floor` (the store-facing outer entry that accepts `repo_root`) or `_apply_completion_floor_to_verdict` (the pure inner function with no `repo_root`); if keeping the inner function, explicitly state that REBAR_ROOT must be set to the tmp store and that AC-3's mutation check must inject a side-effect that uses that env var — or switch the test target to the outer entry point so the fingerprint check is non-vacuous.
findings.finding: The store-fingerprint mutation check is vacuously true for the specific function under test. The plan calls `_apply_completion_floor_to_verdict` directly — a function documented as 'Pure (no LLM)' with no `repo_root` parameter and no store I/O. Any mutation introduced into this function that touches the store would have to use ambient `REBAR_ROOT`; the plan does not state this dependency explicitly. More critically, the plan's claim that the store fingerprint 'catches ANY signing / transition / reopen / event-append the floor might perform' is only defensible if the test exercises the store-facing outer entry point (`_maybe_apply_completion_floor`, which takes `repo_root` and calls `orchestrator.delivered_children_manifest`). Testing the inner pure function with a real store fingerprint check will always pass trivially, making AC-3 ('The test fails if a signing/transition/reopen/event-append is introduced into the floor') unverifiable as specified.
findings.suggested_fix: In step 2, change the tested function from `_apply_completion_floor_to_verdict` to `_maybe_apply_completion_floor` (with `completion_floor_active=True` in a monkeypatched config, `repo_root=<tmp_dir>`, and stubbed LLM classifier returning the gold map). This makes the store fingerprint assertion non-vacuous: `_maybe_apply_completion_floor` calls `orchestrator.delivered_children_manifest` which reads from the store via `repo_root`, so any inadvertent signing/transition call in that pipeline would mutate the store and be caught. Alternatively, if the inner pure function is preferred, add an explicit plan note: 'REBAR_ROOT must be set (not just repo_root=) to the tmp store, because `_apply_completion_floor_to_verdict` has no repo_root parameter — only ambient-env side-effects are caught.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether the store-fingerprint mechanism should wrap `_maybe_apply_completion_floor` (which has `repo_root` and real store access) rather than the pure `_apply_completion_floor_to_verdict`, or replace the real-store approach with a spy/mock on the store-mutating primitives (sign_manifest, transition, etc.) that would actually intercept a mutation introduced into the pure function.
findings.finding: `_apply_completion_floor_to_verdict` is documented as 'Pure (no LLM)' and accepts no `repo_root` parameter — it has zero I/O pathways to the store. The plan's AC step 1 and step 4 specify creating a real rebar store and asserting its BEFORE/AFTER fingerprint is unchanged, but calling this pure function cannot ever mutate a store regardless of what store exists. The detection mechanism described is structurally incoherent with the function's verified contract.
findings.location: What section step 1 & step 4; AC step 1 & step 4

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command or artifact for AC3 (e.g., a pytest parametrize approach with a deliberate-inject fixture, or document the exact mutation + `make test` invocation as a required PR checklist step with its expected output).
findings.finding: AC3 ('The test fails if a signing/transition/reopen/event-append is introduced — verified by a mutation check (temporarily add such a call and confirm RED)') describes a manual developer action with no automated proving command. A CI run cannot produce evidence that this check was actually performed.
findings.suggested_fix: State the exact mutation to apply (e.g., 'add `sign_manifest(...)` inside `_apply_completion_floor_to_verdict` before the return') and the exact command (`make test 2>&1 | grep FAILED`) plus expected output, so a reviewer can reproduce it deterministically.

findings.finding: AC3 ('Test fails if a signing/transition call is introduced') has no mapped step or proving command that demonstrates the RED behavior. An executor who writes passing spies but accidentally writes them incorrectly (e.g., patches the wrong import path) would satisfy AC1+AC4 while AC3 silently fails — the plan provides no command to evidence the RED path.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3: 'Test fails if a signing/transition call is introduced (even via a helper/rename).'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC step such as: 'Adversarial check: temporarily add `sign_manifest(...)` inside the floor function (or a helper it calls), run `pytest tests/unit/test_completion_floor_e2e.py::test_non_regression_floor_is_review_only` and confirm it exits non-zero, then revert.' This can be a manual smoke step or a dedicated xfail sub-test.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the second structural invariant explicitly in step 5 (or correct 'two' to 'one' if only one is intended).
findings.finding: The What section (step 5) says 'Keep the two cheap structural invariants' but lists only one (`attest._MANIFEST_PREFIX == 'plan-review'`). The second invariant is never named, leaving an executor uncertain what to implement or preserve.
findings.suggested_fix: Either list the second invariant explicitly (e.g., a specific constant or function signature check) or change 'the two cheap structural invariants' to 'the one cheap structural invariant'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the second structural invariant explicitly (or correct 'two' to 'one') so the AC is fully specified and the implementer can satisfy it without guessing.
findings.finding: The plan states 'Keep the two cheap structural invariants' but only names ONE (`attest._MANIFEST_PREFIX == "plan-review"`). The identity of the second invariant is unspecified, leaving an implementer unable to faithfully satisfy this AC.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, item 5 / What section last paragraph

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the second structural invariant explicitly in What step 5 (and in the AC if it is to be asserted), or change 'two cheap structural invariants' to 'one cheap structural invariant' if only `attest._MANIFEST_PREFIX == 'plan-review'` is intended.
findings.finding: What section step 5 says 'Keep the two cheap structural invariants' (plural) but names only ONE in the entire plan: `attest._MANIFEST_PREFIX == 'plan-review'`. The second structural invariant is never identified anywhere in the plan text.
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'Keep the two cheap structural invariants' to 'Keep the one cheap structural invariant' if only the `_MANIFEST_PREFIX` check is meant, or explicitly name the second invariant (e.g., `attest._MANIFEST_PREFIX` not appearing in `close_path` source, or the `close_path` reference) so the implementer knows what to preserve.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify and name the second structural invariant referenced in What step 5, and add explicit AC lines covering both named invariants (including `attest._MANIFEST_PREFIX == "plan-review"`) so no described deliverable is orphaned.
findings.finding: What step 5 claims two structural invariants are being kept but names only one (`attest._MANIFEST_PREFIX == "plan-review"`); the second invariant is never specified anywhere in the plan. Additionally, the one named invariant has no covering Acceptance Criterion — the AC list omits any check for preserving this assertion.
findings.location: What section, step 5; Acceptance Criteria section (all 5 AC lines)
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) add two AC lines such as '`attest._MANIFEST_PREFIX == "plan-review"` is still asserted in the new test' and '<second invariant name> is still asserted', or (b) if only one invariant was intended, remove the word 'two' and add a single AC line for the manifest-prefix check.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how `REBAR_ROOT`/`repo_root` is set and restored (e.g., `monkeypatch.setenv` or a context manager) so the env var mutation is scoped to the single test and cleaned up on both pass and fail.
findings.finding: The plan mutates process-level shared state (`REBAR_ROOT` env var or `repo_root` config) to point the process at the temporary store, but specifies no restore/cleanup mechanism. If the implementer uses `os.environ['REBAR_ROOT'] = ...` directly without a monkeypatch or try/finally, other tests in the same process (especially under parallel execution) will inherit the mutated env var.
findings.location: What section, step 1: 'point the process at it (REBAR_ROOT / repo_root)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the plan: 'Use pytest's `monkeypatch.setenv("REBAR_ROOT", str(tmp_path))` to set the store root; monkeypatch automatically restores the original value after the test, even on failure.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm the store-fingerprint implementation uses `snapshot_head()` (or the same EMPTY_REPO_SENTINEL-safe logic from `_concurrency.py`) rather than a bespoke git invocation, to avoid NIH duplication and guard the fresh-repo edge case.
findings.finding: The plan describes capturing 'the tickets-branch HEAD sha' as the store fingerprint, but the codebase already has snapshot_head() in rebar._engine.rebar_reconciler._concurrency for exactly this purpose (reads the configured tickets branch SHA via git_adapter.rev_parse, with EMPTY_REPO_SENTINEL fallback). Not referencing it risks the implementer writing a bespoke git subprocess call that duplicates this existing utility.
findings.location: What section, step 1: 'capture a BEFORE fingerprint of the store: the `tickets`-branch HEAD sha'
findings.suggested_fix: In the new test, import and call `from rebar._engine.rebar_reconciler._concurrency import snapshot_head` and use `snapshot_head(tmp_path)` for the tickets-branch HEAD component of the before/after fingerprint.

findings.finding: The plan specifies spies on 'event-append / file writes', but the three floor functions under test (_apply_completion_floor_to_verdict, _classify_completion, _maybe_apply_completion_floor in src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py lines 229-357) contain no calls to append_event or file-write operations. Installing spies for call paths that don't exist in the functions under test is unnecessary scaffolding (YAGNI).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria line 1: 'Spies assert zero calls to signing/transition/reopen/append and no store mutation escapes the returned verdict dict.'
findings.suggested_fix: Narrow AC1 to the confirmed reachable targets: `sign_manifest`, `transition`, and `reopen`. Drop `append_event` / file-write spies unless a code path from the floor functions to those calls can be identified.

findings.finding: The plan asserts that 'sign_manifest / transition / reopen / event-append' are viable spy targets when running the floor (the three floor functions) over the _partial_epic fixture. A grep of the three floor functions (_apply_completion_floor_to_verdict, _classify_completion, _maybe_apply_completion_floor in plan_review/__init__.py and passes.py) shows that none of sign_manifest, transition, or reopen( appear in those call paths — sign_manifest is only called from attest.py (outside the floor), and reopen/transition are not called anywhere in the plan_review module. Spies placed on these symbols while invoking only the floor functions will show zero calls trivially, providing no stronger guarantee than the source-token test they replace.
findings.location: ## What / Acceptance Criteria (AC1: 'Spies assert zero calls to signing/transition/reopen/append')

findings.finding: AC3 states the spy test 'fails if a signing/transition call is introduced even via a helper/rename', but the plan never specifies WHICH import binding(s) to patch. Python monkeypatching only intercepts the specific binding being patched; a floor helper that imports `sign_manifest` via a different path (e.g., `rebar.signing.sign_manifest` vs. `rebar.sign_manifest` vs. `attest.sign_plan_review`) is invisible to a spy on the wrong binding. This is the same evasion class the plan set out to fix, moved one layer deeper — the spy mechanism only achieves AC3 if the patch target is the binding the production code actually resolves at call time.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3: 'Test fails if a signing/transition call is introduced (even via a helper/rename).'
findings.suggested_fix: In the plan's What or AC section, add: 'Patch sign_manifest at the binding the floor module imports from (e.g., `rebar.signing` or `rebar.llm.plan_review.attest`) so any call through a helper is intercepted. Verify binding correctness with a one-shot adversarial injection: add a bare `signing.sign_manifest("x", [])` call inside `_apply_completion_floor_to_verdict`, run the new test, confirm RED, remove the injection.'

findings.finding: AC2 says 'Before/after store + attestation snapshots are unchanged' but the current test has NO store or attestation snapshots — it only checks source tokens and _MANIFEST_PREFIX. AC2 implicitly introduces NEW snapshot assertions that don't exist today, without specifying what is snapshotted (which store path, which attestation fields) or how.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace AC2 with: 'The in-memory verdict dict is byte-identical before/after a no-drop floor run, and no rebar._commands._seam.append_event call is recorded by the spy.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
